program: C-HERO KAMA intake — image source discovery (vans + EVs)
rights_status_reminder: UNRESOLVED — no downloads performed. All entries below are URLs/metadata only, gathered via WebSearch/WebFetch.

domains_encountered.why: Its own About page self-describes as 'a commercial vehicle export brand' that 'provides electric and fuel commercial vehicles for distributors, fleet operators, logistics companies and project buyers' and 'supports left-hand drive vehicle supply, model selection, export documentation... for qualified partners' — i.e. an authorized export/trading brand, NOT the OEM factory. Notes the OEM relationship: 'In 2010, Shandong Dongfeng KAMA Vehicle Co., Ltd. was established through strategic cooperation between KAMA and Dongfeng.'
domains_encountered.notes: Despite not being the factory, this is by far the highest-quality source found: self-hosted images (zb_users/upload/ CMS paths, not a marketplace CDN), dedicated /news/ articles with precise model-coded titles (ES7, ES8, ESP7, ESP8, EV1/EW1) and dedicated /product/fuel-mini-van/ pages with clean model pages for S6/S7/S8/SP6/SP7/SP8. Category pages under /product/ev-van-truck/, /product/ev-mini-truck/, /product/ev-light-truck/ use generic SEO titles with NO model code confirmation — treat those as unconfirmed-family candidates only.

domains_encountered.why: Self-titled 'KAMA Export Supply & Quality Control Center', explicitly serving 'overseas distributors, importers and fleet buyers' — a trading/QC intermediary, not the factory.
domains_encountered.notes: Has clean product pages (e.g. ESP8) but several embedded images are re-hosted from sc04.alicdn.com (Alibaba CDN), i.e. marketplace-sourced imagery laundered through a branded page.

domains_encountered.why: Search snippets attribute this domain to '山东凯马汽车制造有限公司' / 'Shandong Kama Automobile Manufacturing Co. Ltd.' — the actual OEM factory name.
domains_encountered.notes: FETCH FAILED: certificate has expired. Content could not be verified or read. This is the single most promising candidate for a true manufacturer domain and should be retried once the cert issue clears (or via a tool that can bypass strict TLS verification with founder sign-off).

domains_encountered.why: Titled 'KAMA Vehicles_KAMA Truck', reads as an official-style brand site.
domains_encountered.notes: FETCH FAILED: unable to verify the first certificate. Not explored beyond search snippets.

domains_encountered.why: Search result attributes this to 'Shandong Kaima Automobile Manufacturing Co. Ltd.' (note: 'Kaima' spelling variant) under a page titled 'hengtiankaima'.
domains_encountered.notes: Not fetched — surfaced only in search snippets. Possibly a related/renamed factory entity; needs manual disambiguation before trusting it as core KAMA OEM.

domains_encountered.why: 'Kama Automotive International' — homepage frames itself around '/oem-projects' and serving international buyers; no model-specific pages found (site nav only exposes /products, /oem-projects, /contact-us, /about-us with no deep links surfaced).
domains_encountered.notes: Not useful for this pass — no model pages located.

domains_encountered.why: made-in-china.com marketplace storefronts. Even though the storefront names reference Kama-affiliated export entities ('Shandong Kama Automobile Manufacturing Co., Ltd.', 'KAMA Automobile Import and Export (Chongqing) Company Limited'), the domain itself is a third-party B2B marketplace.
domains_encountered.notes: FLAGGED — will likely fail the rights gate per program instructions. Currently the ONLY source found for EX1, EM31, EM32, EM61, EW2, and several ES/ESP variants (ES6, ESP6, ESP7 supplementary). Images are served from image.made-in-china.com CDN with two resolution tiers per asset (202f0j00... standard, 3f2j00... larger) and carry made-in-china.com branding/watermarking risk.
FLAGGED — surfaced as source for ESP6, ESP7, EM32 listings but not deep-fetched for images since kamaauto.cn/made-in-china already covered those or better manufacturer-adjacent alternatives existed.

kama-serie-s.note_family: Gasoline/CNG mini van & minibus. All candidates below are from kamaauto.cn dedicated model pages — clean, non-marketplace, model-code-confirmed via URL slug and alt text.

S6.estimated_resolution: unknown — no explicit width/height attrs seen; CMS-native upload, likely ≥1200px based on file-size pattern of same-batch site images
S6.notes: Two additional gallery PNGs on the same page (202605111778485993607088.png, ...544526.png, ...417957.png) — likely a 3-4 shot set of front/side angles; re-check angle assignment visually before staging.

S7CNG.model_variant_shown: S7 GASOLINE — NOT CNG. No dedicated S7CNG page or photo found anywhere in this pass.
S7CNG.notes: OPEN QUESTION: S7CNG variant likely shares the S6/S7 unitized body with a CNG badge/tank difference that may not be visually distinct. Flagging as best-available body-match, not a confirmed CNG-spec photo.

S8.angle: 3/4 front (alt text: 'KAMA S8 Gasoline Mini Van')
S8.notes: Two supporting PNGs on same page for alternate angles.

SP6.notes: Page also has side-profile and rear-view PNGs (202605111778483876726928.png side, ...878442423.png rear) — a full 3-angle set exists here, better coverage than most SKUs.

SP8.notes: Full angle set present (front: ...4995543545.png, side: ...4997669317.png, rear/alt-3/4: ...4998141163.png).

kama-serie-es-esp.note_family: Electric van, visually near-identical body to Serie S. Distinguish by EV badging/grille/charge port — NONE of the fetched pages textually confirmed visible charge-port location; needs a visual pass before staging.

ES6.image_url: not fetched — marketplace listing only, image URLs not extracted this pass
ES6.notes: OPEN QUESTION: no manufacturer-adjacent (kamaauto.cn/kamaqc.cn) source found for ES6 specifically. Only marketplace hits (this one, plus 'Kama Es6 Esp6 LHD Rhd Pessenger and Cargo Electric Van' bundling ES6+ESP6 in one listing). Needs a dedicated fetch pass if this SKU proceeds past the rights gate on a marketplace exception, or a deeper search on kamaqc.com once its cert is fixed.

ES7.estimated_resolution: unknown, CMS-native, news-article embed (2025 upload path, likely full-size editorial photo rather than thumbnail)
ES7.angle: unconfirmed — 6 images in article, angles not individually verified visually
ES7.model_variant_shown: ES7, 41.93 kWh, 5.27m, 1.5T payload — explicitly named in article title and (per tool extraction) image captions
ES7.occlusions_watermarks: none reported, but article-embedded images may carry inline caption text — verify no burned-in text crosses the vehicle silhouette
ES7.notes: 5 additional images on same article: 202507231753255574820229.png, ...574413054.png, ...574506450.png, ...575152186.png, ...577338189.png. Best-documented ES-series candidate found — dedicated editorial feature, not a generic listing.

ES8.angle: unconfirmed across the 6-image set (front/side/rear/detail claimed by extraction tool but not independently verified visually)
ES8.occlusions_watermarks: extraction pass reported 'watermark: model title overlay' on each image — VERIFY this is an HTML caption, not a burned-in graphic overlay on the photo itself, before treating as C-HERO clean

ESP6.image_url: not fetched — marketplace listing only, image URLs not extracted this pass
ESP6.notes: OPEN QUESTION: no manufacturer-adjacent source found. Alibaba listing 'Kama ESP6 Electric Passenger 220km Mileage' also exists as a secondary marketplace option, not fetched.

ESP7.image_url: NONE — article confirmed to contain no dedicated ESP7 product photography (only unrelated related-post thumbnails: Nepal Electric Mini Bus, mini cargo pickup, 18-seat passenger van).
ESP7.model_variant_shown: n/a — text-only editorial coverage of ESP7
ESP7.notes: OPEN QUESTION: strongest manufacturer-adjacent text source has zero usable imagery. Marketplace fallbacks not yet image-extracted: 'KAMA ESP7 14-Seat Electric Microbus' (made-in-china, 'Kama Classic Esp7 Van for Passenger 14 Seats'), and Alibaba 'KAMA EV ESP7 Electric Mini Ev Bus 14seats'.

ESP8.occlusions_watermarks: extraction reported per-image caption overlay text — verify not burned into photo
ESP8.notes: Full 3-shot set on this article: side profile (...497269247.jpg), 3/4 rear-side (...517244665.jpg). Second, independent source below.

ESP8.estimated_resolution: unknown, CMS-native (this one at least is self-hosted, not alicdn)
ESP8.occlusions_watermarks: none reported on this specific self-hosted image; NOTE the same page also embeds multiple sc04.alicdn.com images (Alibaba-CDN, marketplace-sourced) mixed in — do not use those, only the two kamaqc.cn-hosted ones (this .jpg and a companion .png technical diagram at ...066830977.png).
ESP8.notes: Independent second source corroborating ESP8 body/badge; useful for cross-checking against the kamaauto.cn version.

kama-serie-ew-ev.note_family: Electric mini flatbed truck. EW1/EW2 spec says RHD — confirmed for EW2 via marketplace listing. EV2/EV3 could not be confirmed as distinct marketed model names anywhere in this pass.

EW1.image_url: NONE — article confirmed to contain no embedded product photography for EV1/EW1, text/spec only.
EW1.notes: Best-named source has no photo. Fallback candidates below are generic 'mini truck' listings on kamaauto.cn's ev-mini-truck category with NO confirmed model code — treat as unconfirmed-family, not EW1-confirmed.

EW1.model_variant_shown: UNCONFIRMED — page titled generically 'Kama New Energy Mini Cargo Pickup Truck', no EW1/EV1 code stated
EW1.notes: Body type (single-cab electric flatbed) plausibly matches EW1/EV1 family per spec sheet, but this is a body-shape guess, not a badge-confirmed match. Spec sheet states 'Left/Right' steering options available — does not resolve LHD vs RHD for this specific photo.

EW2.estimated_resolution: two tiers referenced in URL pattern — 202f0j00 (standard) vs 3f2j00 (larger/original); this is the standard tier
EW2.facing_direction: RHD — steering wheel should read on vehicle's right when viewed from front; verify visually
EW2.model_variant_shown: EW2 confirmed — title states 'Rhd Vehicle', spec table lists 'single cabin(1600)RHD'
EW2.notes: 5 more images at same path with different IDs (...EysoCwijSZgz, ...UyKqCGlcnsgr, ...UnjoCPiFLKup, ...YFZqvcVDEjgr, ...fnKcCZhMksrz). This is currently the ONLY source found with an explicit RHD spec match for EW2 — flagged marketplace, will need rights-gate exception or a manufacturer-domain equivalent search once kamaqc.com/kamavehicle.com TLS issues are resolved.

EV1.model_variant_shown: UNCONFIRMED — generic title '2025 pure electric mini truck with double cabin', no EV1 code stated. NOTE: this is a DOUBLE-CABIN body — EV1 per the queue spec should be single-cabin RHD/flatbed family (EW1/EW2/EV1/EV2/EV3 all listed together); double-cab may actually be a different, unlisted variant. Flag for founder/spec review before treating as EV1.

kama-serie-ex-em.note_family: Heavy electric cargo truck. NO manufacturer-adjacent (kamaauto.cn/kamaqc.cn) source found for any model in this family — every candidate below is a made-in-china.com marketplace listing under the 'kamaqc' storefront (self-described Shandong Kama Automobile Manufacturing Co., Ltd. account name on that marketplace, per its company page — but the domain itself is still made-in-china.com).

EX1.estimated_resolution: standard tier (202f0j00 prefix); larger tier likely available by swapping to 3f2j00 prefix per site pattern seen elsewhere
EX1.model_variant_shown: EX1 confirmed, RHD, tropical-climate export spec, 6+1 wheels

EX1.estimated_resolution: standard tier; 3f2j00-prefixed originals also referenced on page
EX1.model_variant_shown: EX1 confirmed, low-cost variant, 6+1 wheels, MP5 media/A-C
EX1.notes: Second independent EX1 listing — useful for cross-checking body panel/grille details against the first.

EM31.model_variant_shown: EM31 confirmed, light-duty, 6+1 wheels, open cargo box 4160x2100x400mm
EM31.notes: 5 more images: ...CvrcqOSzysuN, ...vBgcqejdPsuy, ...KezcklsBwZpy, ...KBgckFSrwjzN, ...yCrkohsGkKuF. A second EM31 listing exists ('Kama Em31 Bev for Emerging Markets 2.5t Capacity', https://kamaqc.en.made-in-china.com/product/UEhRaOIlgZYc/...) not yet image-extracted.

EM32.estimated_resolution: standard tier; page also referenced higher-resolution '3f2j00'-prefixed gallery variants
EM32.model_variant_shown: EM32 confirmed, 350km range, CATL battery, open cargo box, single cabin 2030mm width
EM32.notes: 5 more images: ...nFskhLRKLorH, ...ONKkVbRhrcgP, ...FnjoWKUBMqzH, ...nySkhAfPkqgD, ...AOZkhRfabqgP. NOTE: extracted image_url is missing its filename suffix in the raw fetch — re-verify exact URL before staging.

EM61.estimated_resolution: 3f2j00 prefix = larger/original tier per site pattern
EM61.model_variant_shown: EM61 confirmed via spec match (360km WLTP range, 4160x2100x400mm cargo box, 6+1 wheels, fast-charging, 70/140kW motor, 140.15kWh battery matches queue's EM61 identifiers) — note the listing TITLE itself is generic ('Kama Factory Product Fast-Charging Electric Truck'), model code is inferred from spec match, not stated in title. Verify before treating as fully confirmed.

open_questions: No confirmed OEM factory domain was successfully accessed. kamaqc.com (attributed in search results to 'Shandong Kama Automobile Manufacturing Co. Ltd.') returned 'certificate has expired' on fetch; kamavehicle.com returned 'unable to verify the first certificate'. Both deserve a retry — possibly the founder can access these directly in a browser where cert warnings can be manually bypassed, to confirm whether either is the true factory site before the rights gate is ratified.

kamaauto.cn, the single richest image source found, explicitly self-identifies as an export/trading BRAND, not the OEM factory. Its images are clean and self-hosted (not marketplace-watermarked), which may still make it a reasonable rights-gate candidate as an 'authorized reseller/distributor' tier — but that is a policy call for whoever ratifies packages/catalog/manufacturer-domains.json, not something this discovery pass can resolve.
S7CNG: no image found showing the CNG-specific badge/tank; only the gasoline S7 was found. If CNG visual distinction matters for the catalog, this needs a dedicated follow-up search.
ES6 and ESP6: zero manufacturer-adjacent (kamaauto.cn/kamaqc.cn) images found — only marketplace hits, and even those weren't image-extracted this pass (listing URLs recorded, image URLs are not).
ESP7: the best manufacturer-adjacent source (kamaauto.cn news article) confirmed to contain ZERO product photography. Only marketplace fallbacks exist and were not image-extracted.

EV1 vs EW1 vs the 'double cabin' variant: the queue lists EW1/EW2/EV1/EV2/EV3 as one family, but the only kamaauto.cn candidate found for 'EV1' is a DOUBLE-CABIN body, which structurally conflicts with the single-cabin flatbed description in the queue's visual_identifiers. This needs founder/spec clarification — it's possible 'EV1' generic photos found are actually a different, unlisted double-cab variant, not true EV1.

EV2 and EV3: no distinct marketed imagery, model pages, or even marketplace listings found under these exact codes anywhere in this pass. They may be spec-sheet-only trim levels of EV1 (battery/range tiers) rather than visually distinct vehicles — worth confirming against the Supabase products table before assuming a photo gap needs filling.
EX2: no marketed imagery, model page, or marketplace listing found under this exact code anywhere in this pass — same open question as EV2/EV3, may be a non-visually-distinct trim of EX1.

Several 'estimated_resolution' fields are genuinely unknown — none of the fetched HTML exposed explicit width/height attributes or CDN resolution-suffix conventions (except made-in-china.com's two-tier 202f0j00/3f2j00 URL-prefix pattern, which is a resolution signal but not a pixel count). Actual pixel dimensions will need a real HTTP HEAD/image-metadata check once download is authorized — none of this was verified by fetching bytes, per the no-download constraint.

Several images across kamaauto.cn news articles were reported by the fetch tool as carrying a 'watermark' that appears to be HTML caption/alt-text rather than a burned-in graphic overlay — this couldn't be verified without rendering the actual image, and should be re-checked visually before assuming C-HERO watermark-free status.

kama.com.cn ('Shandong Kaima Automobile Manufacturing Co. Ltd.') was not explored — surfaced only in one search snippet with a name-spelling variant ('Kaima' vs 'Kama'). Worth a dedicated follow-up to determine if it's a related OEM entity with its own product photography.
